sprint: Real Root Cause Found: Theme Was Applied to the Story Slot, Not the Artwork Slot
task: The genuine root cause behind the persistent white-box report, found via a live browser-console diagnostic (a temporary SlideRenderer.debugResolveBorder export) rather than further speculation: AppState.project.artworkTheme was null on the user's real project, even though the Layout/Holder/Place/Frame-Variation geometry rendered correctly. Both are explained by the same fact -- the World's theme was applied into the Story Theme slot (AppState.project.theme), not the Artwork Theme slot. _resolveBorder/_resolveWallTone required _artworkTheme(s) (which only ever reads the Artwork Theme slot) to be non-null, found nothing, and fell straight through to the legacy _drawPanel(t.panel.color,...) fallback -- painting the Story Theme's plain white/cream panel colour regardless of any earlier fix, since every one of those fixes lived inside the _artworkTheme(s) branch that never ran. Layout/Holder/Place geometry rendered correctly anyway because _activeLayoutHolders/_resolveLayout already resolve through _layoutTheme(s), which tries the Artwork Theme slot first and falls back to the Story Theme slot -- exactly the fallback the chrome-resolution functions were missing. Fixed by having _resolveBorder (both branches) and _resolveWallTone use that same _layoutTheme(s) fallback instead of _artworkTheme(s) directly -- whichever theme actually supplies Layout/Holder/Frame-Variation data now also supplies its own Frame chrome, consistent with what the rest of the Place system already does. Verified via Playwright by reproducing the exact real condition (AppState.project.artworkTheme forced null, AppState.project.theme pointing at the Layout/Holder/Frame-Variation-bearing theme): Place edge pixels now read the wall tone (234,231,226), not solid white (255,255,255). Re-verified Museum Gallery (a theme correctly applied via the Artwork Theme slot) still renders its classic-white Frame Variation pixel-exact and unaffected, plus every prior fix's own reproduction (navy Scene background, dangling Frame Variation reference) still passes. Full regression across the entire existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero console errors. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
